title: Naturalistic fMRI and MEG recordings during viewing of a reality TV show
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous naturalistic fMRI and MEG recordings from 60 participants (30 per modality) who watched a 25-minute excerpt from a Chinese reality TV show. The study captures brain activity during both active viewing with comprehension assessment and subsequent spontaneous mental replay, providing a rich multimodal neuroimaging resource for investigating naturalistic cognition and inter-subject correlation analyses in ecologically valid contexts.
methods_description: fMRI data were acquired on a 3.0 T Siemens Prisma scanner using T1-weighted MP-RAGE anatomical scans (1×1×1 mm voxels) and T2-weighted echo-planar imaging functional scans (2.5×2.5×2.5 mm voxels, TR=2000 ms, TE=30 ms). MEG data were recorded using a 64-channel optically pumped magnetometer system (Quanmag) with 1000 Hz sampling rate and radial sensor orientation. Participants viewed a naturalistic video stimulus followed by comprehension questions and a 15-minute eyes-closed resting period. fMRI preprocessing included skull stripping, motion correction, slice-timing correction, and normalization to MNI152NLin2009cAsym template using fMRIPrep v20.2.0. MEG preprocessing involved artifact removal, independent component analysis, and source localization using structural MRI.

funding_references.funder_name: Shanghai Key Laboratory of Brain-Machine Intelligence for Information Behavior
West China Hospital of Sichuan University Biomedical Research Ethics Committee
